# P-B-003 — Opaque-jurisdiction shopping

> Optimisation de juridiction (paradis opaque)

## Description (EN)

Bidder incorporated in a low-disclosure jurisdiction (BVI, Seychelles, Mauritius, Belize…) with no Cameroonian registry footprint.

## Description (FR)

Soumissionnaire constitué dans une juridiction à divulgation faible (BVI, Seychelles, Maurice, Belize…) sans présence camerounaise.

<!-- END auto-generated -->

## Detection logic

Jurisdiction shopping — beneficial owner sits in a tax-haven jurisdiction (FATF black/grey list) while the operating company is registered locally. Cross-jurisdiction layering depth scores higher.

## Likelihood-ratio reasoning

Default prior 0.30 + weight 0.75 — the FATF jurisdiction list is a regulatory consensus signal; deviation from it is a deliberate decision. K88 Ch.8 — the principal-agent problem amplifies across borders.

## Known false-positive traps

- **Legitimate group structures** for genuinely multinational operations.
- **Tax-haven UBO that is publicly disclosed and tax-treaty-compliant.**

## Production wiring

Requires the UBO adapter chain (ANIF, OpenCorporates) to populate jurisdiction on every Person-link. PEP and sanctions data harden the signal.
